Under warn-only quality mode, a schema-mismatch or other systemic pre-claim intake fault produces the SAME warn fingerprint on every candidate (observed Fizeau 2026-05-18: fingerprint 15155a1c... repeated across distinct bead_ids). The drain loop keeps polling (loop.idle every ~50s) and looks alive, but never claims anything. A 100% identical-fingerprint failure rate is silently masked by warn-only policy; the operator has no signal short of tailing the agent-loop JSONL.

CONTEXT / WHERE
Pre-claim warn decisions are emitted with a stable fingerprint by readinessDecisionFingerprint (cli/internal/agent/readiness_decision.go:23-39), surfaced in the pre_claim_intake.warn event body['fingerprint']. The drain loop lives in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go; the pre-claim intake hook returns PreClaimIntakeResult and the loop already tracks per-bead counters such as consecutive_ladder_exhaustions (execute_bead_loop.go:5348-5398) as a model for cross-iteration accounting.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a cross-iteration counter in the drain loop keyed by the pre-claim warn fingerprint. When the same warn fingerprint is produced on N (default 5, configurable) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes across distinct candidate bead_ids, the loop must:
  - emit a single operator-attention event (kind/event consistent with existing operator-attention emission in execute_bead_loop.go, e.g. around lines 4974/5026) carrying the fingerprint and ONE example payload/detail, and
  - stop looping silently (break the drain / surface a terminal operator-attention state) rather than continuing to poll.
Reset the counter when a different fingerprint appears or a claim succeeds.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the decoder (separate child ddx-606fe854), the rolling claim-success-rate signal (separate child), the CI schema gate, or README docs. Only the consecutive-identical-fingerprint escalation + guard.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. Recommend A (most precise; only neutralizes the spurious symlink->dir case, preserves the guard for genuine edits).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits.
- Do not change what materializes the symlink (external skill tooling) — make ddx resilient to it.

Under warn-only quality mode, a schema-mismatch or other systemic pre-claim intake fault produces the SAME warn fingerprint on every candidate (observed Fizeau 2026-05-18: fingerprint 15155a1c... repeated across distinct bead_ids). The drain loop keeps polling (loop.idle every ~50s) and looks alive, but never claims anything. A 100% identical-fingerprint failure rate is silently masked by warn-only policy; the operator has no signal short of tailing the agent-loop JSONL.

CONTEXT / WHERE
Pre-claim warn decisions are emitted with a stable fingerprint by readinessDecisionFingerprint (cli/internal/agent/readiness_decision.go:23-39), surfaced in the pre_claim_intake.warn event body['fingerprint']. The drain loop lives in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go; the pre-claim intake hook returns PreClaimIntakeResult and the loop already tracks per-bead counters such as consecutive_ladder_exhaustions (execute_bead_loop.go:5348-5398) as a model for cross-iteration accounting.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a cross-iteration counter in the drain loop keyed by the pre-claim warn fingerprint. When the same warn fingerprint is produced on N (default 5, configurable) consecutive pre-claim warn outcomes across distinct candidate bead_ids, the loop must:
  - emit a single operator-attention event (kind/event consistent with existing operator-attention emission in execute_bead_loop.go, e.g. around lines 4974/5026) carrying the fingerprint and ONE example payload/detail, and
  - stop looping silently (break the drain / surface a terminal operator-attention state) rather than continuing to poll.
Reset the counter when a different fingerprint appears or a claim succeeds.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change the decoder (separate child ddx-606fe854), the rolling claim-success-rate signal (separate child), the CI schema gate, or README docs. Only the consecutive-identical-fingerprint escalation + guard.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. The unattended `ddx work` drain halts with "worker exited: operator attention required" when a tracked skill SYMLINK is materialized into a real directory by an external skill-install/sync, leaving the project worktree dirty with a non-tracker path. Observed 2026-05-27 06:26: a continuous ddx worker closed 3 beads, then on the next pre-claim found `.crush/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter` dirty — git tracks it as a symlink (mode 120000) but the working tree had it as a directory (SKILL.md/assets/scripts) — and exited via the uncommitted-implementation-changes guard rather than continue. Operator had to restore the symlink (rm + git checkout) and restart the worker. This breaks the unattended-drain goal: a stray tool-managed skill dir stops the whole queue.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2480 emits "operator attention: project worktree %s has uncommitted implementation changes; released %s" and exits the drain when the landing worktree has any non-allowlisted dirty path (message built at execute_bead_loop.go:955-957).
- cli/internal/agent/work/guard.go:38-48 trackerMetadataPaths only allowlists DDx-managed files (.ddx/beads.jsonl, .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl, …) — added by ddx-df77e668. Tool-managed skill symlinks under .crush/skills/ (and likely .claude/skills, .agents/skills) are NOT covered, so their symlink->dir materialization reads as implementation dirt and halts the drain.

PROPOSED FIX (pick one; implementer/operator decides)
A. Pre-claim: treat a tracked symlink that has been materialized into a directory whose content matches the symlink target as benign (optionally auto-restore the symlink) instead of implementation dirt.
B. Pre-claim: allowlist tool-managed skill-dir roots (.crush/skills, .claude/skills, .agents/skills) in the cleanliness check, analogous to trackerMetadataPaths.
C. Repo hygiene: gitignore + untrack these tool-materialized skill paths so they never read as dirty (note: they are currently committed as symlinks, so this is a deliberate structural change).
Recommend A (most precise; only neutralizes the spurious symlink->dir case, preserves the guard for genuine edits).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not weaken the guard for genuine uncommitted source/test/doc edits.
- Do not change what materializes the symlink (external skill tooling) — make ddx resilient to it.
